text:
I would give ZERO stars if it was an option from customer service to making calls. Paid my bill off and phones want my SIM cards unlocked waited less 24 hours and they said oh no you owe more money paid that and now I have no e mail confirmation saying I own my phones was told by two representatives it would be 72 hours and this last dumb a** said it could take 6 days REALLY!!
Didn’t take 6 days for you to process the money 🤬🤬
T mobile is the worst service. If you're trying to decide which service to go with, run FROM t mobile,at&t or Verizon, both are great !

Okay. So San Diego is basically a big city full of military service members and I’m one of them. The big catch of them getting customers is that they also offer a military discount. But honestly it’s really not worth it to the service that they provide. Sometimes I get full bar and I’m struggling to load anything on social media where I could talk to my friends and family. And having an iPhone, sometimes I can’t even send a simple text message using iMessage. And the company knows that there’s thousands and thousands of military personnel stationed in San Diego who wishes to use their service, but the problem is That the signal is so bad and it’s just not worth it. When I’m on duty, on the ship I get so frustrated just to talk to my family because of how horrible the service is and it may display LTE, but it doesn’t give any service to my phone until I find a “sweet spot” around the ship where I can actually properly communicate with other people. Once I completely pay off my phone I’m about to switch to different networks because it is truly not worth it. Take my word for it and take your business elsewhere.
Awful customer service. They talk nice but all they speak is lies. They make up fake promotions in order to get your business and you find yourself having to call them multiple times a month.

I have been with Tmobile for over 20 years have paid them close to $41,000 when I added it up .In October 2019 I went into the store to add a 5 line was told it was a promotion running for a free 11 iPhone all he had to do was trade in a good phone. I had  8+ so which I did my husband got my 10  r I I took the phone they charge me for the phone when they said they weren’t fought with them back-and-forth come to find out there was no such promotion in the store so T-Mobile which I thought at the time they took three or four payments away for me so I  did not have to pay anything on my bill ,but I thought it was taken care of the phone, only to find out that itWas not and now I have to pay 18 months $31.25 they got my phone 8 plus phone and there was no promotion ,and there’s nothing I can do about it unless they put back my bill of $700 I am so discouraged with this I would not recommend Tmobile to anyone, need to move on.Wow in this day and age with everything going on they really suck
Worst company you can do business with go with someone else

In October 2019, I got cell line with T mobile. It's 1 phone. Over the past 7 months, They have gotten the Bill correct one time. They have been charging me for 2 lines. I've had to go in and get the store manager to correct it. Each time is an hour to hour and a half in the store. I pay the bill on time or early when in the store with the manager. The following month, T-Mobile claims the payment was late though I have the receipts. Then if any of their experts try to make any change, They add on a fee for that the next month. The bill should be about 45.00. The April bill is 96.00 with them claiming I did not pay 28.00 of the last bill thogh done in store with the manager, a 5.00 late invoice fee though the bill was paid on time in the store. At this point, I would recommend any T-Mobile customer should look over all their bill's and see what false charges T-Mobile is placing on them. I would not recommend anyone get a cell contract with T-Mobile.

They still use disingenuous practices to suck every dollar they can. If this company went bankrupt it wouldn't be the worst thing. They will mobile stack their customers. Example i switched over to them from Verizon horrible mistake. Their rep not only set me up with 3 different plans on one account. They shut off my service when I questioned my bill. The only thing they did was apologize while still wanting me to pay for service on 3 different plans when I only asked for one plan. The worst company to do wireless business through. People if you're thinking about using tmobile save your business from this horrible company. Don't do it

They are the worst company ever we have been with them for over 10 years I have a family member that is sick with this virus and all of a sudden my phones are shut  Off I cannot contact them and nobody is there to answer the phone says I'm behind on my payment which I made today and it went through

Signing a contract in 2019 we were told about the outstanding coverage between Columbia, Missouri and Pekin, Il.
Veterans discount was nice but when we left Missouri to come back to Pekin, Illinois our great coverage stopped as well as the great service.
I run a business, and can not get any bars in 5-6 different locations around Pekin ,Illinois.
These locations are the 6 most worked locations that I contract work, no voice, messaging, emailing, nothing.
When I need to talk about my service which is part to nothing with an agent, it's nothing also, I will not recommend this company to any veteran or business owner in the future.We are paying $123.00 a month for a non working phone service but I will end it in a week.Save yourself trouble and money, go to a reliable company that will practice what they preach.Disgusted in Illinois.

I love T-Mobile and have been a T-Mobile customer for many many years. I just wonder who the butt monkey is that ordered the upside down billing envelopes and who the brain dead manager is who said to use them.

We switched to T-Mobile December 2018. Upon receiving the first bill, we noticed we were being charged for protection 360 on 2 of the phones which we did not authorize. On February 22, 2019, I visited the store and spoke with the person who sold the phones and plan to us, Kyle. He said the credit would be on the bill and it would take up to 3 billing cycles to appear. We never received the credit. He also gave me his personal cell number so that I could contact him if the credit did not appear. 

In August 2019, we had to cancel our TMobile plans because my son was starting in college at West Virginia University and there was absolutely no reception with TMobile. I was forced to pay in full for both phones which were a BOGO promotion at no fault of mine. I spoke with so many representatives about this and they repeatedly referred to the contract that was signed and were not willing to bend at all. 

I spoke with Kyle in the store again in August after texting his number with no response. In regard to the refund for the protection 360, which was never removed from the bill, was now totaling $210. His response was that I had cancelled the account and therefore they couldn’t credit me the money owed to me for the plan. After some back and forth, he said he had to have his regional manager take care of the credit. He was going got contact her and let me know.

I visited the store again in December 2019 because I still had not received the refund. Kyle avoided me for a period of time, then went to the back room and closed the door. I asked one of the sales people to let him know I needed to speak to him. He reluctantly came out and looked at the account. He said he saw a credit being processed on my account but didn’t know how much or when I would receive it. He also said at this point I had to call customer service to help any further. 

Tonight, I called customer service. They told me I have to go to the store and request the credit. The store is obviously closed due to COVID 19. 

I’m am at the end of my patience with TMobile. Between being forced to pay for both phones on full ($1500) so that I could communicate with my college son and not receiving the refund )$210) I have requested numerous times for something I did not authorize, I can honestly say I am ready to take legal action at this point. 

Bad move switching to TMobile!!! Don’t do it!

Update: I received a call from a TMobile executive today, one day after reporting them to BBB. I was shocked. They are refunding the overpayment for the protection plan and also refunding a portion of the BOGO deal.

TMobile which, now also owning PCS Metro is cutting people off during the Pandemic.  Wow, way to be totally unhelpful in this time of need when, people must use their phone to communicate for numerous issues.

As a business, we have had T-mobile for 5+ years for our ~10 employees.   We have two business locations (one in a downtown metropolitan area) and neither location gets service.  We had to install boosters in our main location just to get service.  Our employees also travel abroad 75% of the time and one employee has not been able to get a signal for over two weeks now because T-mobile contracts with Airtel in that country and there is no service in her metropolitan area.  Because of the terrible service issues and long hold times with business care, I do not recommend T-mobile.

Orlando Area supervisor Miguel Ramirez is useless they could not solve me.  They canceled my cell spot without authorization and told me that I had to return it to the store and it was their mistake and that's why I pay more than $ 100 per month and I have no signal at home.  T mobile does not work.

T-mobile has great customer service as long as you are patient and kind! They helped me during the Covid-19 crisis and delayed payments on my business lines for several months. I have been with T-mobile for ten years and they reward loyalty every time I need to contact them. They also helped me change my phone number at no cost. I have multiple business lines with T-mobile and I salute them for offering the additional features to everyone for free during the Covid-19 crisis unlike other phone carriers. 5 stars!
Don’t bother calling Customer service.  You call a third world country and they know nothing.  As a customer, they ask you to trouble shoot.  DUMBIES

slow internet unless you live next to antenna, if you live in the center of P.R. some places no service.Right now with epidemic ultra slow internet. Prices sky high! Not happy at all! Wish i was..but no!
Tuesday gifts are like cheap toys for children thats if any are left. (Drove 2 hrs for a tin cup)

On a Sunday it was 10 mins till 11am. The lady running the store, in the plaza at Babcock Rd., with HEB was outside smoking a cigarette. Told me to come back at or after 11am. So Fine I went to HEB and came back 10 minutes later or 11:10am. Well the door was still locked and she was on the phone inside and waved me off or, to wait. It was April 4th so there were not a ton of customers looking for a phone due to the Corona virus. I've had less than good impressions from them before. Well this is the last TIME! 

I'd like to buy the One Plus 7T and I have the necessary requirements to find another phone company.

Unhappy in San Antonio,

T Mobile is disconnecting phone service during Covid-19 global pandemic. Today is 4-4-2020 and they disconnected my husbands BUSINESS phone service. He has been a BUSINESS customer with T-Mobile for over 5 years. The way how a company treats their customers as well as their employees during this sensitive GLOBAL time, will define that companies brand for decades to come and quite frankly I'm disgusted by the stance they are taking. I was honestly interested in switching my service over to them from AT&T but after seeing how they treated him, I will NEVER.

T-Mobile is the worst phone service out there do not get this phone company they are very rude and if you try to make changes to your account they are no great help even if you disconnect with them before your bill cycle is over with they still charge you a full month highly hate this company never will I get this company ever again

I had TracFone with service via Verizon.  There were holes as Verizon through TracFone is not the same as for Verizon customers.  So I decided to upgrade both my phones and service with T-Mobile.  That was a huge mistake.  From my personal experience, T-Mobile's data coverage sucks.  Their coverage map shows total coverage in areas where I got zip, zero, nada for a data signal, and that was in areas where I had good coverage with TracFone.  I am paying at least four times as much for cellphone service now, and getting less.

Update as of 01/22/2023
So much has changed since my first review.  Coverage is exponentially better.  I even had my first chance to use their international service.  It was not terrific, but good enough to stay in touch with contacts and happenings back home.  I would move my rating today up to 4 stars.

Tmobile is a great company.  I've been with them for 17 years.  Like every company they have some issues but they are still the best.  If you are having problems ask for a supervisor and don't take no as an answer.  It's worth it.

Amazing group here at T-mobile, always wanting to help and looking for the best interest of the customer. 
Customer service is amazing!
Product and service is amazing!

Looking forward to see T-mobile grow. If there is a merger on the horizon, here is to hoping they stick to the T-mobile way of doing business.

Piece of s*** company. after 10 years with them I canceled because of coverage issues. I canceled with them more then 2 years ago, and conveniently ,or incompetency,they only canceled 2 out of 3 lines. no usage on the 3rd line. took multiple calls in 2 months period to cancel the 3rd line. Every time I called they said they didn't had a record of any previous calls.  They passed me to collection company.
I honestly like T-Mobile service. My gripe is with their policy of report phones lost. I bought a PAID OFF phone and then months later they still allowed the original owner to call the phone in lost.

Do not get T-mobile. It is pure trash. It took me about an hour just to get this page to load. I wish I could say I was exaggerating. At first, the salesmen told us everything we wanted to hear. We got free Apple Watches for switching and a free tablet. Every Tuesdays they have a bunch of sales. So, if you’re looking for free stuff, this is what you go for. But you pay for what you get. The salesman failed to mention how trash the service is. When we switched to T-mobile, I couldn’t get the network to work for two days. Even after that, the service was very poor. So, we got an antenna that was supposed to speed the connection up. It did absolutely nothing. I’m fact, my family had it for about a week, and nobody noticed a difference. For some reason, every night around midnight (with no parental controls set), the cellular network shuts down. I then have to switch to the WiFi and then that shuts off as well. So then I have to switch back to the data. This service is absolutely awful.  Working from home due to the Coronavirus has made it difficult with trash network to get things done, but it was this way before the outbreak. Only now, the outbreak has caused me to work from home more, using more data. So after a certain amount of data has been used, they slow the speed down. Therefore, they slow an already slow speed down to practically nothing. Everyday I have to restart my phone just to get a connection. Don’t waste your money nor your time. :)

I wish there was a (-) rating too. I bought a brand new phone with a brand new number. Right after 15-20 days or whatever was the time frame to return my phone, i started having problems with the service when I connect my phone to a WiFi. The Second i connect it to a WiFi, i simply no longer receive any sms or calls at all. Plus İ can not make an online payment or through TMobile app or even an online rep can not make a payment. i have to go a Tmobile store every single time. I ask them why this is happenin ? Guess what they say? Yes u guessed it right. None of  TMobile employees has an single idea what to do and I'm told to send the phone to the original company and pay from my own pocket for a replacement. What a scam what a ripp off what a shame. Shame on you t mobile. I went to your store to buy an iPhone and your employee direct me to this phone called (one plus) claiming it is same quality  and for a less price. Such a liars. Now i just realized that I had missed messages and calls for two days from the company i work for and my side customers telling me that they can not reach me for 2 days. I'm out of words for your lack of respect to customers.

I had T-Mobile for about 3 years. I dreaded every time I had to deal with customer service because I knew it was going to be a bad experience. The last few months I had them, I was running out of data the first day of my cycle, I’d have 1-2gigs coming out at 3am when I know I was sleeping. I told them about this problem and they said I’d have to pay for more data. I told them that if that was the case I was leaving. They said, “bye, have a nice day.” Will never go back. Will never give them another red cent. I hope that Verizon, att, and sprint will eventually run them out of business.

I am a long time customer of T-Mobile. My problem is not with their service it is awful. I get service everywhere but at work. I have stayed with them (17 years) because of the price. Long story short I pay for my mothers phone service since she is on a fixed income and my income is limited as well. This company treats their existing customers like trash, while they lavish new customers with free Netflix, and Napster music subscriptions. Lets not forget to mention the free phones. I have contacted customer service to voice my concerns, but Habib never forwards my concerns to the Phone Gods. (language barrier I guess) I just want to warn others before they make a mistake and switch to T-Mobile.
Exceptional service was received from Ms. Selina at the 730 Gulfgate Center location.  Was extremely pleased with the customer service that was rendered.

DO NOT BELIEVE THEM WHEN THEY SAY SOMETHING IS “FREE”. They will take you for everything you own. Took advantage of me when I didn’t know any better and now I’m stuck paying over $1000 for a “free” phone and they are not giving me anything for the $1200 phone I traded in. They are unapologetic sharks out for your money even if you barely have any like me
T-Mobile f-ing sucks I don’t recommend it to anyone having this provider has been the worst experience of my life DONT GET TMOBILE

I canceled my service with T-Mobile. Awesome service everywhere else expect my house. I couldn’t do it anymore. I had to switch. I called in to cancel and talked to someone in customer service. I paid off both phone and the remainder of whatever else we owed. I asked more than once if everything was paid off and that everything was taken care of. Customer service told me yes.. multiple times...I got a bill in the mail for almost $200. Not only a bill but another bill from collections. I called in 2 days ago and they put my account under investigation. I will take you to court if this doesn’t get fixed. I won’t be going back.

METRO PCS BY T Mobile sucks!  I WILL MAKE SURE DAT I WILL BE SHARING DIS REVIEW WITH ALL OF THE SOCIAL MEDIA OUTLETS AS WELL AS THE NEWS CHANNEL!I woke up dis morning only to find out dat my phone services was interrupted by t Mobile. In a time of crisis this company turn my phone services off. With all dis going on in the world do to the coronavirus outbreak this company is so uncaring with no compassionate wats so ever. My grandmother & grandfather lives in  New York where the virus has the worst cases & the hardest hit area do to dis virus.  Both of my grandparents has CONTRACTED the virus and are in critical condition and we don't know if they are going to pull thru. My phone is the only way i can contact the hospital as well as other family do to the social distanting we all hath to do to keep safe. This is the only way people can stay in contact with their family & love ones. I can't believe that t Mobile would be so cold hearted &careless at a time like dis. I thought we were all in dis together as the world claims. Well apparently T MOBILE must have forgotten the memo about care & being there for its customers.  Fyi on your website says you are there for all your employees and customers but its a complete lies. You are not there. I know dat you & everyone else are watching the News about dis coronavirus and dat everyone is in need & need each other's support. Why would you interrupt someone's phone services knowing dat dis is the only way to communicate with family and friends; shame on you T Mobile. As soon as I start back to work and get some funds im indeed changing my phone services to another company! I wish dat i didn't have to pull any star because you guys are about money , not supporting & caring for others accept  for your own selfish reasons! ;( ;(;(

The worst company I ever see in my life, I had just wanted ask about my bill- because they increased my bill with out any reason- I was in hold for 2 hours 😡and finally nobody answer the hone .....,😡😡😡😡

Went in on 1/16/20 to store #9378 Columbia S.C . to switch from verizon to tmobile. Talked  to Tanner and Hannah who told my wife and myself about their magenta 55 plan which was suppose to be $70.00 a month taxes and fees included. My wife had an iphone 7 and i had a  a nice flip phone we wanted to use was told they could not use the flip phone  . They told us  about their special offer to trade in her iphone7 for a iphone 11(cheaper version)  i am old school and wanted another flip phone which i would have to pay for but they had a free (their words) samsung A10e. Their iphone11 was according to them $700 and $50 to upgrade the memory, but they would allow her $500 trade in leaving a $250 balance add in to otter cases and two screen protectors.We left the first  day after paying $27.70 to get them activated because we had an appointment that we were late for, told them i would return the next day to pay the balance on the phone and otter cases and screen protectors. The next day went back and told them i wanted to pay off the balance on all equipment they said it would be $346.61. which i paid. Here is where i will take the blame for my trusting them, they showed me on their device the receipt showing paid in full. NEVER leave the store without a  printed receipt, my mistake. Customer service said i could review that receipt on line and it would  show the itemized receipt of what i paid for , another LIE. The bill is now $84.16 per month so i guess we are paying everything twice. Lesson learned and my my advice to you is DO NOT deal with tmobile

The worst experience ever!  Customer service absolutely do not know what they are doing or saying! They have no credibility whatsoever! Trying to cancel my account for the last 3 months and could not do it! Lost access to online billing and been told by representatives do not pay and wait till the end of the cycle for several times and ended up with collector calls! Spent many useless hours with them on the phone. Do not go with T-Mobile....

Lies lies lies!!!! First they said they would pay off current carrier- lies.

Said i get a free phone- lies!

Im in debt almost $1,000 because of them.
Don't believe a thing they say. 
Its all about SALES!!!!!!!

About two months ago I switched to T-Mobile after 20+ years with Verizon, to take advantage of an over 55 flat rate plan for $55.

TLDR: They sell the service, but can't deliver the service.  Avoid.

My experience so far has been that the store at Apple Blossom 
Mall in Winchester fumbled transferring the service to my phone and setting up my account.  Along with inconvenient incompetence, they added some kind of $15/Mth protection plan without my permission which I discovered on my first bill.  Fine, I called billing and got it taken off.

Service was working OK up till about 5 days ago, Then, I suddenly started losing voice and text service for hours at a time. It comes back for 10 or 15 minutes, then disappears again.  I get no service available messages, dropped calls or just clicks without connecting.  Text messages are taking between 2 and 8 hours to be sent. Responses take as long to receive.

I live alone and am in that 60+ higher risk group, so when I can't call an ambulance or the fire department, it sorta concerns me (ya think?).

They have absolutely no service support, NADA. It's a phone company who does not want you calling and bothering them when the phone service isn't working. Lots of ways to pay your bill online, but report service outage? Nope.

I had to create a twitter account, and then tweet a complaint. 15 minutes later I get a canned looking response from some guy who says "DM" me.  After I figure out what that means, and how do do it, I discovered his message queue was set to refuse all direct messages.

Another complaint, and another 15 min pass.  New person says same thing but her mailbox worked.  She asked about the problem, and that I follow a link and verify the account.  I did all that immediately and then, nothing. I sent a "Still here" message after 1/2 hour and got nothing.  After and hour and 10 minutes I get a text from T-Mobile support (Hey It's working for a change!) saying to keep their employees happy they are working reduced hours so...I guess the online support person just went home or what ever.

All this took from 9:30PM to 12:30AM.

It's like the Seinfeld joke about the car rental reservation, They can sell the service, they just don't know how to deliver the service.

I am going to assume because of so many extra people at home, they have nowhere near the capacity to both stream everybody's Netflix movies and my voice calls, so maybe it would be fine normally, but if I can't count on them in an emergency, they are not the right company for me. 

I am very sorry I switched, and am now trying to get back on Verizon without going into a store.

This is a terrible product. No one either at their stores or at their service call in centers has any idea how to make these work. I am soooo sorry that I left ATT for mobile phones (due to excessive cost for three lines) and left Vonage (again, high cost for two home lines). Don't trust T-Mobile!  Their service tech insulted me - told me that I inserted the SIM card upside down - that is impossible as it is polarized by a notch in one corner.  I have called 11 times to their 800 number from my old Vonage home line and 611 numbers from my iPhone 11. After 9 of these calls, three trips to T-Mobile stores, and no call backs twice on the last two calls, another anomaly:  now my iPhone 11 has no service while my wife's iPhone 11 (bought at the same time and entered service at the same time) has only one or two bars.  It has been over a month and still not working on one of my two home lines.  The only reason one of the two home lines is working is because I went to the local store and insisted on a new sim card - the guy at the store scanned the SIM card bard code and that one started working when I plugged it in at home.  I suspect that a poorly trained person on my last call that went through to the T-Mobile service group, somehow turned off my iPhone instead of turning on my Line Link SIM card.  
Jim Cramer has had the President of T-Mobile on his Mad Money show at lest twice and Cramer seemed to support their success as a company for investment purposes.  To that I say RUN AWAY!  The walls at T-Mobile may come crashing down because of their shoddy product and services and this will affect their ability to grow and show a profit.  
If this review site had any lower rating than one star, I would have chosen an even lower rating,  This product and their services are an F.  Do not buy this or anything else from T-Mobile!  Demand better!

Switching over from AT&T to t mobile on hold 3 hours on Friday finally got a person and did switch, Saturday got e mail from AT&T said our phone number was no longer associated with them, now have no  service. Was 9n hold for 5 hours on Saturday, NEVER got through! So today was on hold for 4 hours and then was cut off. Can't go on line as the don't recognize my phone number or e mail. Chated and they tell me to call, no phone for 3 days and all the stores around are closed, really feed up!!

I have been with T-Mobile for 14 years a very loyal customer. Since the coronavirus started T-Mobile has jumped ship you cannot get a hold of them you cannot talk to anybody everything is automated on your T-Mobile website if you don't have access to that you are even more screwed. I am so upset and disgusted with T-Mobile for abandoning their customers when they needed them the most, during a national crisis people need to be able to get a hold of each other. There was a huge Verizon store right across the street from my neighborhood and I noticed they were open so I went in there and I switch to Verizon after 14 years with T-Mobile. I think the people at Verizon care about their customers because they know they need to stay in touch during this national crisis. And that means more to me right now than anything. I would like to say one thing to T-Mobile. Shame on you T-Mobile, you really let us down you let all of your customers down you let America down for not being there when they needed you most.

BUYERS BEWARE!  Took my tablet in to T-Mobile Store #9231 in Corpus Christi TX to purchase a SIM card for data use while in Texas for the month.  My tablet was not SIM card capable and salespeople tried to sell me a new tablet for $700+.  Said I would not spend that kind of money on a tablet. They showed me a couple different tablets.  Said I did not want to spend money on an 8" tablet.  Male salesperson, I had three talking and laughing with me and, as I now discover, distracting me, said they had a promotion for a alcatel Joy Tab for free.  All I had to do was pay the taxes.  I can remember him laughing and joking and commenting that you know you have to give Uncle Sam his share. I used the tablet while in Texas and on our trip home.  I continued to use the tablet until I found an unlocked 10" tablet I wanted to purchase.  I was going to cancel the data service for the tablet to find I still owe over $150 on the tablet.  I was duped.  I fell for their scheme.  I should have read every damn line on the screen before signing it.  It was not a paper contract.  It was on a small screen.  He showed me just enough to see the price of $20 for the monthly data and the $10 for the SIM card, plus the tax for the tablet.  I had two friends with me who witnessed this. I am furious with myself, but more upset with T-Mobile.  Their mobile service is about the same as others.  Pros and Cons.  My husband has Verizon and has much better service than my T-Mobile service.  We are retired and travel quite a bit. There are many places I do not have service.

I've been a T mobile customer for years without problems, a few months back their app couldn't perform the simple task of paying my bill, you know, the only reason for having a companies app.  I then started using there site to pay my bill, or at least tried, unlike every other website I use you can't simply store your username and password and unlike every other website there's no simple method of recovery.  I had to wait an hour two days ago to speak with someone who could help, she did, I was able to reset my password and clicked "update password" problem solved right?  NO!  Tried logging in today and not only was my username and password not stored the password I KNOW I CHANGED IT TO doesn't work.  Seriously I just wanted to pay my bill with ease and they made it such a pain that I'm now on hold with them (for 37 minutes and counting) on my new Verizon phone.  FYI I logged into my Verizon account with ease and it even stored my username and password on both the app and the website, what an incredibly novel concept.

I was lied to by the store associate
customer service promised that they would take care of the situation & call me back when  they did I was told they couldn't. The store associate tacked on cell phone insurance and tethering onto my plan without me asking. T-Mobile also routes new customers support calls to domestic call centers for the first couple months to make you think that they don't have overseas call centers.

Tried to set up an account to set up a payment plan, but whatever password you offer, it gets rejected. To get anyone to talk to you, you need account information you can't even attain! So now I'm gonna lose my phone service. On what planet is this fair?

Been a Tmobile Business customer for ovet 6 years. Bill and coverage has never been an issue., until now. Since, the latest updates my services have degraded. Slow internet, slow connections time to calls, dropped gps. Did the whole troubleshooting thing. Replaced my SIM card and it's still has not fixed the problems.  I'm not about to pay for an upgraded phone when the service is not reliable. I'm giving t-mobile a few more weeks and if the services do not improve if I'm switching to Verizon. They have plenty of honest, true deals for new customers.

Phone Customer Service is HORRIBLE !!!!
Tried calling several days in a row and was on hold for over an hour each time and the Local store doesn't even pick up!!!
Bad service, I will never open a line with you again . You’re treating your customer’s in a rude way!

Y'all are lying, 3 weeks ago y'all sent me a promo and my wife and I started an on-line business and we started to make money. We get paid on the internet, you said y'all would help with the virus but unless you have money right now, no you will not help, you don't care even on your site there is no help you just ask for money for donations to help your CEO is a liar my wife and I were 2 paychecks away from actually making it after the bad wreck I had last year cause me to lose my job, my house, and everything I owned. I simply asked to have my internet restored for 24 hours so I could get paid and you couldn't do it because I wasn't a customer yet. Now you sent me a hot spot and I was 9n the cusp of making it. I even asked to turn it on for 2 hours to collect my money and you couldn't do that. My phone credit is messed up because I had AT&T for years and I could not pay my bill because of the wreck I had. So now I am stuck. No T-Mobile did not help they do not care unless you have money, do not tell the American people you are doing everything you can to help cause you are not I don't care what your CEO says cause he is lying 2 hours is all we asked and you would have got your money 2 hours and you couldn't give us that. I know my comments mean nothing to T-Mobile or anyone else it won't change anything but I do know that someone in this country may look at what I have said and think damn 2 hours they couldn't give someone 2 hours in a time of crisis what are they going to do to us when things are normal. Nothing is what they are going to do, I take that back, they definitely know how to apologise really well for why they don't care

DO NOT USE T MOBILE !!! On March 3rd, I signed up for the Senior plan for 2 lines and 2 free phones for $55. per month; taxes and fees included, and then $5 off for Auto Pay. I ended up shelling out $93.00 : $20 each one time fee for Activation, and $24 each phone one time fee for Samsung A10E phones. T MOBILE engages in Unfair and Illegal Business Practices. Not a week later, my bill arrived and it was for $86.40 ??? NO !! I have the original add , AND, On Friday, the 13th, I was in the Hospital, and the representative Bella, sent around 40 emails one right after the other,(within 10-15 mins) knowing and full aware I was in the Hospital !!! This is harassment via Federal line. When I spoke with Stephanie TODAY, she said they would have to ship me 2 other phones to correct my account??? WTH. NEGATIVE!! I already have my photos, contacts, etc. on this new phone as well as new phone cases and screen protectors. Just adjust my bill the way it is supposed to be. You will never substitute a cheap phone for the one the AD and agreement included !!! SO, I am also filing a Formal Complaint with the Consumer Protection Division of the Attorney General. Shame on these LIARS and THIEVES.

I am very pleased to say John187780 Has an amazing customer service! Will defenitley continue my service thank you tmobile for having such a great staff!!

Absolutely one of the worst phone companies out there... NO REALLY. You have better luck using a payphone because they have no service anywhere even when you have full bars your texts and call wont go through or will drop. Absolutely horrible .

TERRIBLE COMPANY. I SHOULDNT NEVER SWITCHED FROM METRO. I NEVER HAD A PROBLEM WITH METRO. YESTERDAY MY DATA RAN OUT, THE REP TOOK MY MONEY AND THE NTOLD ME HE COULDNT GIVE ME MORE DATA. I went in to talk about switching from Verizon to T-Mobile.  I was offered a great plan with new IPhone11.  Went back 2 days later and talked to the same sales consultant and was told that he made a mistake and offered me something that he was not supposed to.  He refused to keep the original offer but apologized.  I called customer service and they also refused to keep the original offer.  The difference for them was $300.  This is bad customer service...just lost a customer over $300.

I went to T Mobile in Eau Claire Wisconsin and had a young man who helped me his name is Cole he went above and beyond to help me, he was very polite and the nicest young man ever. He deserves a raise for sure. I have a hard time with the technical stuff and he explained it to me, helped me get all set up and did a fantastic job. So thank you Cole you're the best.
T-mobile is a great company they assist you with what you need and if they take long to assist you they are just helping somebody else.

Called for tech help because my phone goes straight to voice mail.  I was twice asked if I had the volume turned up. Then the customer service rep decided to call me to confirm that I had the volume turned up.If that wasn't enough, the customer service rep insisted that I play music on my phone when I told her the speakers work.  I was told to go to a T Mobile store to get help. Really? The clerks at the stores are supposed to fix my phone, not a tech? How insulting...asking me more than once if I had the volume on, calling me to see if the phone really wasn't ringing through, etc. I am an older adult, but apparently that disqualifies me from knowing if my cell phone volume is turned up, whether my speakers work, etc. Gosh, how do I get out of bed everyday and drive to work? The state of California trusts me to teach college, but T Mobile thinks I am not capable enough to turn on the volume of my phone. Wow, what ageism!

I would not even give it a one star and if I had the opportunity it would be a dead zero. This company is the most ridiculous company to work with I have ever experienced in my entire life! Our service never got connected and they charged us anyway and took over two months till they promised to send our money back and they still never did. We had to have our bank reverse the charges to Get our money back. And now we just received a statement charging us that fee plus a $35 return fee!!!?...this company is absolutely the worst customer service!!!!no one can help us!!!....we have had to wait over two hours during several phone calls just to get on the line with those people at T-Mobile. I will tell every single person I know never to use T-Mobile! We even went down to a retail store to have them help us as well and we were there an hour and a half with them trying to figure out how to get our money back. The operator there from some other country suggested we go on a prepaid plan just so they didn’t have to lose the commission from the sale back in December .... they promised our money back we did not get our money back until I requested it from my bank in March!!!
On hold for over 10 minutes, Call took almost 45 minutes. Great call other than the wait time. 4761026 did a wonderful job!!!!

Well reading all these reviews pretty much scare me. I hope this is not my experience as I am a new T-Mobile customer my husband and myself. I need to say that store 157D in Deerfield Beach Florida is awesome. Dasia and Michael were the best.. they did everything with a smile and they were patient with my husband and myself. I hope to God that these reviews are not true. I guess more will be revealed but for right now I'm a happy new customer to T-Mobile.

My experience was actually 5 stars up until daylight savings. I'm an Arizona based customer, and most of Arizona does NOT participate in daylight savings. However, T Mobile changed my time anyways. So, now my phone displays the wrong time and I do NOT want to unselect the automatic date and time option because I'm a traveler that travels a lot. This type of mistake is unacceptable and inexcusable and is good reason for dropping your rating from 5 stars to one star. Please fix this issue and I would be happy to edit my review back to 5 stars with a pleasant review to read to go along with the 5 stars. Would greatly appreciate it.

Tmobile has the worst coverage I've ever had to deal with. Its beyond infuriating; we are in the year 2020!! We shouldn't be having to worry about connection issues on our phones with 5G about to drop. When 5G drops if Tmobile doesn't step up their game, Verizon or AT&T here I come.
Their team of customer service screw up my like transfer and cant seem to figure it out! After being on hold on and off for 40 minutes they hung up on me and now my sim card doesn't have service.

They are robbing customers. Charging 20$ per line for reconnect even if you only pay 10$ a month for one line they are still charging 20$ to restore service so for 1 line /1 side line you’ll be paying 60$ or more to restore the line. Plus your past due amount or phone bill. It’s very seldom you find one customer service agent that actually cares to help you.

I switched from Verizon a couple of months ago. Had multiple issues. I have 5 lines. My house got hit with a tornado 5 days ago. 15 tornadoes hit Tennessee. T Mobile offers free Netflix. I had it separately but they Provided it free of charge. Power and cable lines are down. I tried using my mobile hotspot to watch Netflix. Got notice from T Mobile they would be dialing my hotspot speed down since I had maxed it out . Called customer service to see if they could do something under the circumstances. I was told no by two separate customer service reps. The first one and then her supervisor who called to see if I understood everything PJ told me. Buyer beware. I’ll be switching back to Verizon as soon as my house situation improves.

T-Mobile is the worst phone company ever
These people are liars, always apologize for their actions never do nothing to change it....do not fall for it.

Ive been on a plan with my dad for years, and finally decided to get my own line. Superb customer service, and we know customer service is EVERYTHING!! This is THE place!

I’m not sure why the other reviews are so negative, I just switched from Verizon to T-Mobile & not only did they get me out of my contract but they credited me every dime + then some so that it covered the cost of my current bill. I am giving a one rating because I cannot select 0, otherwise my rating will be zero. I have been with Tmobile for 15 years. I have seen the great service in the beginning and the service levels getting bad and worse over years. I had to leave because there was no promotions for loyal customers compared to new customers. Before I quit I called the Tmobile customer service asking about this and they told me they would give all these promotions to new people to attract them to join but they don't care about keeping the loyal customers happy. To top it all, when I left Tmobile I was charged almost twice for my last month bill. The reason I was given was I left Tmobile. So they want to penalize a loyal customer like me for leaving? The customer service was very very rude when I called to ask why the charges are not the normal charges I used to see. The explanation I was given was, because you left. That is the most ridiculous thing I have heard in my life. You can understand my rating now.

I signed up with service over the phone on 03/02/2020. I had a great representative. It was such a smooth process and i was really excited. I received my device in the mail on 03/04/2020. I called to activate the line and i was told that the sim card was terminated. No one knew what was going on. I was transferred to multiple departments. I was on the phone for three hours just being transferred around.I had to repeat my story over and over. It was so frustrating. I finally just said i wanted to cancel, send the phone back and be reimbursed. It was a long process but eventually it was finished. My refund showed in my account as pending. When i looked at my bank statement today, the charge posted again. I called customer service and again no one new what was going on. So again, i was being transferred around. No one seemed to care about it. This has really been a nightmare and
now i am out on money and a phone. Very disappointing.  Hopefully no one else will have to go through this situation. I don't even know if my account was actually cancelled. I have no idea!
The worst service out there' driving down the road?deadzone Walk into a building? Deadzone. They need to sell the company to att or Verizon so it'll be a legit phone company.

I have been a t-mobile customer for 3+ years and I really enjoy my phone. I have a Stylo 4 and I stopped by a T-mobile store (6305 Richmond Hwy) just to look around and I saw the Stylo 5. So after my inquiries, I purchased the Stylo 5.  While tranferring my information from one phone to the other, the salesperson (@Register #2) became so irritated. I had a lot of apps and pictures on my phone and it presented a problem in transferring everything. I offered to delete some apps and some of my pictures but the salesperson decided he knew what apps should be removed. His irritation overwhelmed him so much that he did not explain what he doing and he was talking very low because I kept having to ask him to repeat what he was saying.  He did not transferred all my pictures and muttered something about my pictures were under Google. I felt as though he was trying to get me out of the store. T'mobile has many customers of all ages including 60+ years old. T-mobile salesperson need training on how to assist and help all ages. Because this is UNACCEPTABLE!!!

I signed up on 2-8-2020 to get a prepaid line for my child. I was given a phone my friends dad.  However, day 2 I was having issues. I called the Customer Service. I explained that I wasnt still able to use the phone. The Rep was real nice and said the phone had a block and to get a new device. I purchased a new phone and it still didn't work.  I calledon Feb 17 and another rep said the SIM is defective. He suggested I go to the store and swap out the SIM. I left T Mobile 2 hours ago (2-29-20) and bought yet another phone. They made sure it worked before I left. So, I called Customer Service and got someone who was ueless. She couldnt understand why I wanted to get a credit on my bill. She said she saw where the new SIM just got updated. So, Im confused as to if the SIM was just issued how can I have used it for 2 days. She swears it wasnt in use for 14 days. I know I can count from the 8th to the 29 is 21 DAYS. I will be going back to my old company.
